title: GitHub Review by gndrmnn
summary: The reviewer wants two brittle lexical/keyword-based tests removed: lines 3900–3928 in `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller_test.go` and lines 752–790 in `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap_test.go`, since source code shouldn't be tested by string matching and script loading is already covered elsewhere.

code_comments.body: We do not want to test source code lexically. Remove this lexical test (lines 3900 - 3928)
code_comments.author: gndrmnn
code_comments.resolution: Removed the `TestReconcile_DatabaseTLSOrderedBeforeDBConnectionSecret` function (the lexical source-grep test spanning the cited 3900–3928 range) from operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller_test.go, and dropped the now-unused `os` import.

code_comments.body: We already test if the script is being loaded. Testing each keyword as presented here is too specific and brittle. Remove this test (lines 752-790)
code_comments.author: gndrmnn
code_comments.resolution: Removed the `TestBootstrapDBSeedScript_EmbeddedContent` function (the brittle keyword-by-keyword assertions spanning the cited 752–790 range) from operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap_test.go; script loading remains covered by the existing pre-insert-script test.

resolution_summary: Both lexical/keyword tests called out by the reviewer were removed. In keystone_controller_test.go the `TestReconcile_DatabaseTLSOrderedBeforeDBConnectionSecret` test (which read keystone_controller.go and searched for substrings) was deleted along with the now-unused `os` import. In reconcile_bootstrap_test.go the `TestBootstrapDBSeedScript_EmbeddedContent` test (which asserted specific Python keywords inside the embedded script) was deleted; the surviving `TestBuildBootstrapJob_PreInsertScript_ParsesSSLDSNParams` already proves the embed is wired. No additional files beyond the two named by the reviewer were touched.
